ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located (e.g., Europe, Asia) */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Geographical latitude of the country */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Geographical longitude of the country */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language(s) of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_region FOREIGN KEY ("region") REFERENCES regions ("region_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "sub_region" TEXT /* Sub-region within the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health statistic record */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the statistics */,
  "fertility_rate" REAL /* Fertility rate for the year */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Life expectancy for the year */,
  "population" REAL /* Population for the year */,
  "fertility_rate_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the fertility rate measure used */,
  "life_expectancy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the life expectancy measure used */,
  "data_quality_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data quality indicator */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "population_unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population measurement unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_statistics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fertility_rates" (
  "fertility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fertility rate entry */,
  "rate_description" TEXT /* Description of the fertility rate measurement */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Measurement unit for fertility rates (e.g., births per 1,000 women) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fertility_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "life_expectancies" (
  "life_expectancy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each life expectancy entry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the life expectancy measure */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Measurement unit for life expectancies (e.g., years) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("life_expectancy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_units" (
  "population_unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population unit entry */,
  "unit_description" TEXT /* Description of the population measurement unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL for more information about the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality indicator */,
  "quality_indicator" TEXT /* Name of the quality indicator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the quality indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistical_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistical_reports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution affiliated with the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the statistic being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES health_statistics ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the analysis model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the model was created */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis model (e.g., regression, machine learning) */,
  "input_parameters" TEXT /* Input parameters used for the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the model used for prediction */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country for which the prediction is made */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the prediction is made */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value (e.g., fertility rate or life expectancy) */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval for the prediction */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES analysis_models ("model_id")
)
